title: Principal Investigator — MeshAnyOrder for Life Sciences
description: Leading a 7-member independent research collaboration developing MeshAnyOrder, an order-agnostic autoregressive transformer for point-cloud-conditioned 3D mesh generation. The model represents faces as quantized tokens and predicts unvisited adjacent faces from arbitrary traversal seeds; current work extends it with 3D rotary positional encoding, triangle/quad tokenization, topology-aware validity constraints, frontier-parallel decoding, and local completion/remeshing. Publication-grade ablations span random, axis-based, BFS/DFS traversal; causal, adjacency-aware, and bidirectional masking; reconstruction, manifoldness, watertightness, latency, memory, and high-resolution scaling.

title: Principal Investigator — TBXT / Brachyury Small-Molecule Discovery
description: Leading an 11-person chordoma-focused computational hit-identification project targeting PDB 6F59 chain A / TBXT G177D site F. The workflow compresses 2,274 prior-art compounds, 737 raw analogs, 503 filtered analogs, 30,000 BRICS recombinations, and 67 QSAR-pass proposals into a 570-compound novelty-filtered pool, then applies docking, GNINA CNN rescoring, RF/XGBoost QSAR, Boltz-2 co-folding, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, T-box paralog selectivity, Rowan affinity analysis, PAINS/sourceability filters, and Bash/HPC automation.

title: PRISM AI Safety Fellow — Protein Foundation Model Red-Teaming
description: Selected from 800+ applicants for PRISM 2026 to develop adversarial evaluation methods for protein foundation models. The work tests biological plausibility, sequence–structure–function consistency, structural validity, developability, uncertainty calibration, claimed mechanisms, and failure behavior, and builds safety-bounded benchmarks and taxonomies for high-stakes protein-design workflows.

title: ChromoGen-Engine V2 — Conditional Diffusion for 3D Chromatin
description: Developing a conditional diffusion and evaluation pipeline that merges ChromoGen-style single-cell 3D chromatin generation with small-molecule perturbation data and same-cell multi-omic regulatory readouts. The data engine integrates Plate-C / Easy Dip-C structures with CHARM-style Hi-C, RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and histone-modification data under leakage-aware splits and structure-grounded benchmarks.

title: A*STAR V2M-Engine — Autoimmune Target Discovery
description: Architecting a calibrated genetics-to-mechanism target-discovery engine that maps autoimmune GWAS and fine-mapped variants to regulatory programs, causal immune cell states, and targetable mechanisms. The project combines single-cell foundation models, QTL/colocalization evidence, known therapeutic controls, retrodiction benchmarks, perturbation validation, counterfactual cell-state transport, and structure/affinity-aware prioritization.

title: Pedal AI — Chemistry Research-Implementation and Backend Engineering
description: Designed Python/LangGraph and FastAPI infrastructure connecting chemistry agents with molecular-prediction engines, supporting stateful orchestration, model/data evaluation, and inspectable retrosynthesis workflows. Built chemist-facing UI/UX around Coley Lab / ASKCOS-style route search, reaction-level provenance, and decision-ready synthesis planning.

title: ChemAgent-QSM — LLM and Statistical Mechanics/Theoretical Chemistry
description: Developed an LLM-guided agentic framework that converts natural-language chemistry prompts into auditable PySCF workflows for electronic structure, optimized geometries, descriptors, vibrational spectra, time-correlation functions, local order, relaxation, mobility, and structure–dynamics coupling; also developed trajectory features and ML analysis for dynamical heterogeneity.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Antibody–Antigen Interactions
description: Co-leading a six-person AI@MIT / AIM Labs project developing a structure-informed cross-attention transformer and ESM-2 pipeline that maps protein/pathogen sequences to candidate antibodies using OAS, SAbDab, and IEDB data. PPO against AlphaFold-Multimer/IGFold metrics and distributed PyTorch DDP on MIT SuperCloud reduced training time by approximately 40%.

title: EVEdesign — Protein Design Algorithm Engineering
description: Contributing to EVEdesign's open-source, method-independent protein-design platform as part of a 21-person, 18-institution, 8-country collaboration. Work includes uncertainty-aware candidate selection, sequence–structure–function objective integration, protein-language-model/evolutionary priors, agent-based tool orchestration, and scalable lab-in-the-loop biosequence design.

title: AI/ML for Statistical Mechanics Simulations
description: Building a PyTorch/GNN-augmented Kadanoff renormalization-group framework for automatic fixed-point discovery and thermodynamic phase-boundary mapping in spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic systems after ranking in the top 1% of graduate-level 8.334 while in high school; also mentored 200+ students through condensed chemistry, mechanics, and career-path instruction.

title: Kupcinet-Getz Scholar — Computational Biochemical Diffusion
description: Selected as one of 22 scholars worldwide to develop PINN- and neural-ODE-enhanced stochastic simulation of nonequilibrium enzyme reaction-diffusion networks, including custom Euler-Maruyama, Runge-Kutta, and Gillespie SSA solvers. Built GNN encoders, transformer sequence models, UMAP clustering, and classifiers for bifurcation and nonlinear-dynamics discovery in high-dimensional trajectories.

title: Sepal AI / Mercor — Chemistry LLM Evaluation
description: Developed model-training, testing, and evaluation data for graduate-level chemistry reasoning, including retrosynthesis, reaction mechanisms, and method selection. Designed grading rubrics, chemical-validity constraints, instructional data, prompt-engineering workflows, error analysis, and structured fine-tuning/evaluation formats.

title: Computational Organic Chemistry — DFT to kMC
description: Engineered an automated multi-scale pipeline linking density-functional-theory calculations to stochastic kinetic Monte Carlo models; screened 20+ transition states and 500+ trajectories across five solvent pathways, producing a 22% predicted yield optimization for a cytotoxic target and a mechanistic manuscript under PhD mentorship.

title: Advanced Organic Synthesis — Buchwald Lab
description: Supported development of CuH-catalyst chemistry for selective asymmetric methylation and alkylation of vinyl boronate esters, used 1H-NMR and chiral-HPLC to relate catalyst structure to activity, expanded substrate scope, and scaled synthesis from 0.1 mmol to multi-gram quantities.

title: MIT 6.1200[J] Teaching Fellow and Grader
description: Selected after receiving an A+ and ranking in the top 1% of MIT EECS's largest foundational theoretical-computer-science course; supports a 250+ student cohort through recitations, office hours, proof-intensive grading, midterms, and finals in logic, graph theory, recurrences, asymptotics, and cryptography.

title: MIT BioMakers — Computational / Dry-Lab Activity
description: Computational and dry-lab participation in MIT's student-led biomaking community and interdisciplinary biology project infrastructure.

title: Schwarzman College of Computing Advisor
description: Student-advisory participation around computing education, AI, and undergraduate experience at MIT.

title: MIT Student Council Representative
description: Undergraduate student representation and campus-governance participation at MIT.

title: ClubChem
description: Participation in MIT's undergraduate chemistry community.

title: MIT Gala — Model / Designer
description: Model and designer participation in MIT Gala.
